rawAwsError: ValueError: ClickHouse query memory must be at most 5 GiB

rawErrorSource.source: immutable archive result JSON re-read from the exact run-owned S3 archive bucket

diagnosis: Attempt 21 successfully deployed the unchanged standard Phase 7 topology, passed minimal service/TLS/ClickHouse smoke and produced two identical deterministic fingerprints for exactly 15,000,000 source rows. The Phase 7 entrypoint supplied 6 GiB, but the inherited Phase 6 ArchiveConfig still rejected every value above 5 GiB before issuing an archive query. Attempt 20's operational-headroom fix therefore did not reach the actual archive engine validation boundary.

fixDirection.archiveQueryEnvelope: Keep the Phase 6 default at 5 GiB, accept caller-selected query memory through 6.5 GiB and retain at least 512 MiB below the 7 GiB ClickHouse server ceiling. The current Phase 7 caller remains 6 GiB; exact point equality is not acceptance.
fixDirection.cleanup: ECS rejects UntagResource after a task reaches STOPPED. Continue exact-owned runtime/image deletion, classify the stopped task as an immutable Tagging API tombstone and poll it to zero instead of blocking deletion.
fixDirection.failureEvidence: CloudWatch Logs GetLogEvents is not pageable through get_paginator. Follow nextForwardToken until stable so future failure bundles retain logs and CloudTrail evidence.

sourceDropNonExecutionBasis.retainSourceOverride: The sealed controller supplied ARCHIVE_RETAIN_SOURCE_AFTER_COMMIT=true to its only archive RunTask call.
sourceDropNonExecutionBasis.failureOrdering: ArchiveConfig.validate rejected the 6 GiB value before export, COMMITTED creation or any source DROP path.

cleanup.initialRawError: InvalidParameterException: The specified task is stopped. Specify a running task and try again.
